task_completion.description: Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles are applied to the appropriate sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Navigation Pane on the left side of the Word window. It should display a hierarchical outline with 7 main headings (Executive Summary, Sections 1-5, Appendices) and 17 subheadings indented beneath them (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1).

task_completion.description: Document is divided into three sections with correct page numbering in the footers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the footers in Print Layout view. The Title Page should have no page number. The Front Matter (Executive Summary, TOC) should have lowercase Roman numerals (e.g., 'i', 'ii') centered in the footer. The Body (Sections 1-5, Appendices) should have Arabic numerals (e.g., '1', '2') centered in the footer, starting at 1.

task_completion.description: An auto-generated Table of Contents is inserted after the Executive Summary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a standard Word Table of Contents block (often with a 'Contents' heading and a bounding box when clicked) on a new page immediately following the Executive Summary page.

task_completion.description: Auto-numbered captions are added to all 3 tables and 3 figure placeholders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the data tables and the gray rectangle images (480x50 pixels). They should have Word caption text (e.g., 'Table 1', 'Figure 1') positioned directly above or below them.

task_completion.description: Text placeholders are replaced with live cross-references.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the body text where placeholders like '[see Table 1]' or '[see Figure 1]' were located. The brackets should be gone, replaced by the cross-referenced text (e.g., 'Table 1'), which may appear with gray field shading if the cursor is on it.

task_completion.description: A Table of Figures is inserted after the Table of Contents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a list of figures and/or tables with their corresponding page numbers, located immediately after the Table of Contents.

task_completion.description: The document is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Word title bar at the top of the screen. It should display 'environmental_compliance_final.docx - Microsoft Word'. Alternatively, look for this file in a File Explorer window under Desktop\WordTasks.

integrity.description: The agent used the Microsoft Word 2010 GUI to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Microsoft Word 2010 application open with the document being actively edited, rather than a terminal window running a Python script (e.g., python-docx) to manipulate the file.

integrity.description: The agent used Word's native features for TOC, Captions, and Cross-references rather than typing them manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The TOC and Table of Figures should look like standard Word field blocks (often with a light gray background or bounding box when clicked). The References tab should be visible in the ribbon history.

integrity.description: The agent did not delete or alter the core text of the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: The main paragraphs, table data, and overall content of the report should remain intact, with changes limited to formatting, spacing, headers/footers, and reference fields.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document has exactly 7 Heading 1 targets (Executive Summary, Sections 1-5, Appendices) and 17 Heading 2 targets (1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, Appendix A, Appendix B). There are 3 tables and 3 gray rectangle images (figures) that require captions. The 6 cross-reference placeholders to be replaced are '[see Table 1]', '[see Figure 1]', '[see Table 2]', '[see Figure 2]', '[see Table 3]', and '[see Figure 3]'. The final file must be named 'environmental_compliance_final.docx'.
